{
  "name": "context-mode",
  "version": "1.0.89",
  "type": "module",
  "description": "MCP plugin that saves 98% of your context window. Works with Claude Code, Gemini CLI, VS Code Copilot, OpenCode, and Codex CLI. Sandboxed code execution, FTS5 knowledge base, and intent-driven search.",
  "author": "Mert Koseoğlu",
  "license": "Elastic-2.0",
  "keywords": [
    "mcp",
    "model-context-protocol",
    "claude",
    "claude-code",
    "gemini-cli",
    "vscode-copilot",
    "opencode",
    "openclaw",
    "codex-cli",
    "context-window",
    "sandbox",
    "code-execution",
    "fts5",
    "bm25"
  ],
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/mksglu/context-mode"
  },
  "homepage": "https://github.com/mksglu/context-mode#readme",
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": [
      "./build/openclaw-plugin.js"
    ]
  },
  "bugs": "https://github.com/mksglu/context-mode/issues",
  "main": "./build/opencode-plugin.js",
  "exports": {
    ".": "./build/opencode-plugin.js",
    "./plugin": "./build/opencode-plugin.js",
    "./openclaw": "./build/openclaw-plugin.js",
    "./cli": "./cli.bundle.mjs"
  },
  "bin": {
    "context-mode": "./cli.bundle.mjs"
  },
  "files": [
    "build",
    "hooks",
    "configs",
    "insight",
    "server.bundle.mjs",
    "cli.bundle.mjs",
    "skills",
    ".claude-plugin",
    ".openclaw-plugin",
    ".mcp.json",
    "openclaw.plugin.json",
    "start.mjs",
    "scripts/postinstall.mjs",
    "README.md",
    "LICENSE"
  ],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "tsc && node -e \"if(process.platform!=='win32'){require('fs').chmodSync('build/cli.js',0o755)}\" && npm run bundle",
    "bundle": "esbuild src/server.ts --bundle --platform=node --target=node18 --format=esm --outfile=server.bundle.mjs --external:better-sqlite3 --external:turndown --external:turndown-plugin-gfm --external:@mixmark-io/domino --minify && esbuild src/cli.ts --bundle --platform=node --target=node18 --format=esm --outfile=cli.bundle.mjs --external:better-sqlite3 --minify && esbuild src/session/extract.ts --bundle --platform=node --target=node18 --format=esm --outfile=hooks/session-extract.bundle.mjs --minify && esbuild src/session/snapshot.ts --bundle --platform=node --target=node18 --format=esm --outfile=hooks/session-snapshot.bundle.mjs --minify && esbuild src/session/db.ts --bundle --platform=node --target=node18 --format=esm --outfile=hooks/session-db.bundle.mjs --external:better-sqlite3 --minify",
    "version-sync": "node scripts/version-sync.mjs",
    "version": "node scripts/version-sync.mjs && git add .claude-plugin/plugin.json .claude-plugin/marketplace.json .openclaw-plugin/openclaw.plugin.json .openclaw-plugin/package.json openclaw.plugin.json .pi/extensions/context-mode/package.json",
    "prepublishOnly": "npm run build",
    "dev": "npx tsx src/server.ts",
    "setup": "npx tsx src/cli.ts setup",
    "doctor": "npx tsx src/cli.ts doctor",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "test:watch": "vitest",
    "benchmark": "npx tsx tests/benchmark.ts",
    "test:use-cases": "npx tsx tests/use-cases.ts",
    "test:compare": "npx tsx tests/context-comparison.ts",
    "test:ecosystem": "npx tsx tests/ecosystem-benchmark.ts",
    "install:openclaw": "node -e \"if(process.platform==='win32'){console.error('OpenClaw install requires bash (Git Bash or WSL)');process.exit(1)}else{require('child_process').execSync('bash scripts/install-openclaw-plugin.sh',{stdio:'inherit'})}\"",
    "postinstall": "node scripts/postinstall.mjs"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@clack/prompts": "^1.0.1",
    "@mixmark-io/domino": "^2.2.0",

31	
32	## The Problem
33	
34	Every MCP tool call dumps raw data into your context window. A Playwright snapshot costs 56 KB. Twenty GitHub issues cost 59 KB. One access log — 45 KB. After 30 minutes, 40% of your context is gone. And when the agent compacts the conversation to free space, it forgets which files it was editing, what tasks are in progress, and what you last asked for.
35	
36	Context Mode is an MCP server that solves all three sides of this problem:
37	
38	1. **Context Saving** — Sandbox tools keep raw data out of the context window. 315 KB becomes 5.4 KB. 98% reduction.
39	2. **Session Continuity** — Every file edit, git operation, task, error, and user decision is tracked in SQLite. When the conversation compacts, context-mode doesn't dump this data back into context — it indexes events into FTS5 and retrieves only what's relevant via BM25 search. The model picks up exactly where you left off. If you don't `--continue`, previous session data is deleted immediately — a fresh session means a clean slate.
40	3. **Think in Code** — The LLM should program the analysis, not compute it. Instead of reading 50 files into context to count functions, the agent writes a script that does the counting and `console.log()`s only the result. One script replaces ten tool calls and saves 100x context. This is a mandatory paradigm across all 12 platforms: stop treating the LLM as a data processor, treat it as a code generator.
41	
42	<a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QUHrntlfPo4">
43	  <picture>
44	    <img src="https://img.youtube.com/vi/QUHrntlfPo4/maxresdefault.jpg" alt="Watch context-mode demo on YouTube" width="100%">
45	  </picture>
46	</a>
47	<p align="center"><a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QUHrntlfPo4"><img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/%E2%96%B6%EF%B8%8F_Watch_Demo-YouTube-FF0000?style=for-the-badge&logo=youtube&logoColor=white" alt="Watch on YouTube"></a></p>
48	
49	## Install
50	
51	Platforms are grouped by install complexity. Hook-capable platforms get automatic routing enforcement. Non-hook platforms need a one-time routing file copy.
52	
53	<details open>
54	<summary><strong>Claude Code</strong> — plugin marketplace, fully automatic</summary>
55	
56	**Prerequisites:** Claude Code v1.0.33+ (`claude --version`). If `/plugin` is not recognized, update first: `brew upgrade claude-code` or `npm update -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code`.
57	
58	**Install:**
59	
60	```bash
61	/plugin marketplace add mksglu/context-mode
62	/plugin install context-mode@context-mode
63	```
64	
65	Restart Claude Code (or run `/reload-plugins`).
66	
67	**Verify:**
68	
69	```
70	/context-mode:ctx-doctor
71	```
72	
73	All checks should show `[x]`. The doctor validates runtimes, hooks, FTS5, and plugin registration.
74	
75	**Routing:** Automatic. The SessionStart hook injects routing instructions at runtime — no file is written to your project. The plugin registers all hooks (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PreCompact, SessionStart) and 6 sandbox tools (`ctx_batch_execute`, `ctx_execute`, `ctx_execute_file`, `ctx_index`, `ctx_search`, `ctx_fetch_and_index`) plus meta-tools (`ctx_stats`, `ctx_doctor`, `ctx_upgrade`, `ctx_purge`, `ctx_insight`).
76	
77	| Slash Command | What it does |
78	|---|---|
79	| `/context-mode:ctx-stats` | Context savings — per-tool breakdown, tokens consumed, savings ratio. |
80	| `/context-mode:ctx-doctor` | Diagnostics — runtimes, hooks, FTS5, plugin registration, versions. |
81	| `/context-mode:ctx-upgrade` | Pull latest, rebuild, migrate cache, fix hooks. |
82	| `/context-mode:ctx-purge` | Permanently delete all indexed content from the knowledge base. |
83	| `/context-mode:ctx-insight` | Personal analytics dashboard — 15+ metrics on tool usage, session activity, error rate, parallel work patterns, and mastery curve. Opens a local web UI. |
84	
85	> **Note:** Slash commands are a Claude Code plugin feature. On other platforms, type `ctx stats`, `ctx doctor`, `ctx upgrade`, or `ctx insight` in the chat — the model calls the MCP tool automatically. See [Utility Commands](#utility-commands).
86	
87	<details>
88	<summary>Alternative — MCP-only install (no hooks or slash commands)</summary>
89	
90	```bash
91	claude mcp add context-mode -- npx -y context-mode
92	```
93	
94	This gives you the 6 sandbox tools without automatic routing. The model can still use them — it just won't be nudged to prefer them over raw Bash/Read/WebFetch. Good for trying it out before committing to the full plugin.
95	
96	</details>
97	
98	</details>
99	
100	<details>
101	<summary><strong>Gemini CLI</strong> — one config file, hooks included</summary>
102	
103	**Prerequisites:** Node.js 18+, Gemini CLI installed.
104	
105	**Install:**
106	
107	1. Install context-mode globally:
108	
109	   ```bash
110	   npm install -g context-mode
111	   ```
112	
113	2. Add the following to `~/.gemini/settings.json`. This single file registers the MCP server and all four hooks:
114	
115	   ```json
116	   {
117	     "mcpServers": {
118	       "context-mode": {
119	         "command": "context-mode"
120	       }
121	     },
122	     "hooks": {
123	       "BeforeTool": [
124	         {
125	           "matcher": "run_shell_command|read_file|read_many_files|grep_search|search_file_content|web_fetch|activate_skill|mcp__plugin_context-mode",
126	           "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "context-mode hook gemini-cli beforetool" }]
127	         }
128	       ],
129	       "AfterTool": [
130	         {
131	           "matcher": "",
132	           "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "context-mode hook gemini-cli aftertool" }]
133	         }
134	       ],
135	       "PreCompress": [
136	         {
137	           "matcher": "",
138	           "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "context-mode hook gemini-cli precompress" }]
139	         }
140	       ],
141	       "SessionStart": [
142	         {
143	           "matcher": "",
144	           "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "context-mode hook gemini-cli sessionstart" }]
145	         }
146	       ]
147	     }
148	   }
149	   ```
150	
151	3. Restart Gemini CLI.
152	
153	**Verify:**
154	
155	```
156	/mcp list
157	```
158	
159	You should see `context-mode: ... - Connected`.
160	
161	**Routing:** Automatic. The SessionStart hook injects routing instructions at runtime — no `GEMINI.md` file is written to your project. All four hooks (BeforeTool, AfterTool, PreCompress, SessionStart) handle enforcement programmatically.
162	
163	> **Why the BeforeTool matcher?** It targets only tools that produce large output (`run_shell_command`, `read_file`, `read_many_files`, `grep_search`, `search_file_content`, `web_fetch`, `activate_skill`) plus context-mode's own tools (`mcp__plugin_context-mode`). This avoids unnecessary hook overhead on lightweight tools while intercepting every tool that could flood your context window.
164	
165	Full config reference: [`configs/gemini-cli/settings.json`](configs/gemini-cli/settings.json)
166	
167	</details>
168	
169	<details>
170	<summary><strong>VS Code Copilot</strong> — hooks with SessionStart</summary>
171	
172	**Prerequisites:** Node.js 18+, VS Code with Copilot Chat v0.32+.
173	
174	**Install:**
175	
176	1. Install context-mode globally:
177	
178	   ```bash
179	   npm install -g context-mode
180	   ```
181	
182	2. Create `.vscode/mcp.json` in your project root:
183	
184	   ```json
185	   {
186	     "servers": {
187	       "context-mode": {
188	         "command": "context-mode"
189	       }
190	     }
191	   }
192	   ```
193	
194	3. Create `.github/hooks/context-mode.json`:
195	
196	   ```json
197	   {
198	     "hooks": {
199	       "PreToolUse": [
200	         { "type": "command", "command": "context-mode hook vscode-copilot pretooluse" }
201	       ],
202	       "PostToolUse": [
203	         { "type": "command", "command": "context-mode hook vscode-copilot posttooluse" }
204	       ],
205	       "SessionStart": [
206	         { "type": "command", "command": "context-mode hook vscode-copilot sessionstart" }
207	       ]
208	     }
209	   }
210	   ```
211	
212	4. Restart VS Code.
213	
214	**Verify:** Open Copilot Chat and type `ctx stats`. Context-mode tools should appear and respond.
215	
216	**Routing:** Automatic. The SessionStart hook injects routing instructions at runtime — no `copilot-instructions.md` file is written to your project.
217	
218	Full hook config including PreCompact: [`configs/vscode-copilot/hooks.json`](configs/vscode-copilot/hooks.json)
219	
220	</details>
221	
222	<details>
223	<summary><strong>Cursor</strong> — hooks with stop support</summary>
224	
225	**Prerequisites:** Node.js 18+, Cursor with agent mode.
226	
227	**Install:**
228	
229	1. Install context-mode globally:
230	
231	   ```bash
232	   npm install -g context-mode
233	   ```
234	
235	2. Create `.cursor/mcp.json` in your project root (or `~/.cursor/mcp.json` for global):
236	
237	   ```json
238	   {
239	     "mcpServers": {
240	       "context-mode": {
241	         "command": "context-mode"
242	       }
243	     }
244	   }
245	   ```
246	
247	3. Create `.cursor/hooks.json` (or `~/.cursor/hooks.json` for global):
248	
249	   ```json
250	   {
251	     "version": 1,
252	     "hooks": {
253	       "preToolUse": [
254	         {
255	           "command": "context-mode hook cursor pretooluse",
256	           "matcher": "Shell|Read|Grep|WebFetch|Task|MCP:ctx_execute|MCP:ctx_execute_file|MCP:ctx_batch_execute"
257	         }
258	       ],
259	       "postToolUse": [
260	         {
261	           "command": "context-mode hook cursor posttooluse"
262	         }
263	       ],
264	       "stop": [
265	         {
266	           "command": "context-mode hook cursor stop"
267	         }
268	       ]
269	     }
270	   }
271	   ```
272	
273	   The `preToolUse` matcher is optional — without it, the hook fires on all tools. The `stop` hook fires when the agent turn ends and can send a followup message to continue the loop. `afterAgentResponse` is also available (fire-and-forget, receives full response text).
274	
275	4. Copy the routing rules file. Cursor lacks a SessionStart hook, so the model needs a rules file for routing awareness:
276	
277	   ```bash
278	   mkdir -p .cursor/rules
279	   cp node_modules/context-mode/configs/cursor/context-mode.mdc .cursor/rules/context-mode.mdc
280	   ```
281	
282	5. Restart Cursor or open a new agent session.
283	
284	**Verify:** Open Cursor Settings > MCP and confirm "context-mode" shows as connected. In agent chat, type `ctx stats`.
285	
286	**Routing:** Hooks enforce routing programmatically via `preToolUse`/`postToolUse`/`stop`. The `.cursor/rules/context-mode.mdc` file provides routing instructions at session start since Cursor's `sessionStart` hook is currently rejected by their validator ([forum report](https://forum.cursor.com/t/unknown-hook-type-sessionstart/149566)). Project `.cursor/hooks.json` overrides `~/.cursor/hooks.json`.
287	
288	**Known limitation:** Cursor accepts `additional_context` in hook responses but does not surface it to the model ([forum #155689](https://forum.cursor.com/t/native-posttooluse-hooks-accept-and-log-additional-context-successfully-but-the-injected-context-is-not-surfaced-to-the-model/155689)). Routing relies on the `.mdc` rules file instead of hook context injection.
289	
290	Full configs: [`configs/cursor/hooks.json`](configs/cursor/hooks.json) | [`configs/cursor/mcp.json`](configs/cursor/mcp.json) | [`configs/cursor/context-mode.mdc`](configs/cursor/context-mode.mdc)
291	
292	</details>
293	
294	<details>
295	<summary><strong>OpenCode</strong> — TypeScript plugin with hooks</summary>
296	
297	**Prerequisites:** Node.js 18+, OpenCode installed.
298	
299	**Install:**
300
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-22 23:18:05Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/Users/shivpat/context-mode/README.md`

```text
300	
301	1. Install context-mode globally:
302	
303	   ```bash
304	   npm install -g context-mode
305	   ```
306	
307	2. Add to `opencode.json` in your project root (or `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json` for global):
308	
309	   ```json
310	   {
311	     "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
312	     "mcp": {
313	       "context-mode": {
314	         "type": "local",
315	         "command": ["context-mode"]
316	       }
317	     },
318	     "plugin": ["context-mode"]
319	   }
320	   ```
321	
322	   The `mcp` entry registers the 6 sandbox tools. The `plugin` entry enables hooks — OpenCode calls the plugin's TypeScript functions directly before and after each tool execution, blocking dangerous commands and enforcing sandbox routing.
323	
324	3. *(Optional)* Copy the routing rules file. OpenCode lacks a SessionStart hook, so the model needs an `AGENTS.md` file for routing awareness:
325	
326	   ```bash
327	   cp node_modules/context-mode/configs/opencode/AGENTS.md AGENTS.md
328	   ```
329	
330	   This tells the model which tools to use and which commands are blocked. Without it, hooks still enforce routing — but the model won't know *why* a command was denied.
331	
332	4. Restart OpenCode.
333	
334	**Verify:** In the OpenCode session, type `ctx stats`. Context-mode tools should appear and respond.
335	
336	**Routing:** Hooks enforce routing programmatically via `tool.execute.before` and `tool.execute.after`. The optional [`AGENTS.md`](configs/opencode/AGENTS.md) file provides routing instructions for model awareness. The `experimental.session.compacting` hook builds resume snapshots when the conversation compacts.
337	
338	> **Note:** OpenCode's SessionStart hook is not yet available ([#14808](https://github.com/sst/opencode/issues/14808)), so startup/resume session restore is not supported. Compaction recovery works fully via the plugin.
339	
340	Full configs: [`configs/opencode/opencode.json`](configs/opencode/opencode.json) | [`configs/opencode/AGENTS.md`](configs/opencode/AGENTS.md)
341	
342	</details>
343	
344	<details>
345	<summary><strong>KiloCode</strong> — TypeScript plugin with hooks</summary>
346	
347	**Prerequisites:** Node.js 18+, KiloCode installed.
348	
349	**Install:**
350	
351	1. Install context-mode globally:
352	
353	   ```bash
354	   npm install -g context-mode
355	   ```
356	
357	2. Add to `kilo.json` in your project root (or `~/.config/kilo/kilo.json` for global):
358	
359	   ```json
360	   {
361	     "$schema": "https://app.kilo.ai/config.json",
362	     "mcp": {
363	       "context-mode": {
364	         "type": "local",
365	         "command": ["context-mode"]
366	       }
367	     },
368	     "plugin": ["context-mode"]
369	   }
370	   ```
371	
372	   The `mcp` entry registers the 6 sandbox tools. The `plugin` entry enables hooks — KiloCode calls the plugin's TypeScript functions directly before and after each tool execution, blocking dangerous commands and enforcing sandbox routing.
373	
374	3. *(Optional)* Copy the routing rules file. KiloCode shares the OpenCode plugin architecture and lacks SessionStart, so the model needs an `AGENTS.md` file for routing awareness:
375	
376	   ```bash
377	   cp node_modules/context-mode/configs/opencode/AGENTS.md AGENTS.md
378	   ```
379	
380	4. Restart KiloCode.
381	
382	**Verify:** In the KiloCode session, type `ctx stats`. Context-mode tools should appear and respond.
383	
384	**Routing:** Hooks enforce routing programmatically via `tool.execute.before` and `tool.execute.after`. The optional [`AGENTS.md`](configs/opencode/AGENTS.md) file provides routing instructions for model awareness. The `experimental.session.compacting` hook builds resume snapshots when the conversation compacts.
385	
386	> **Note:** KiloCode shares the same plugin architecture as OpenCode, using the OpenCodeAdapter with platform-specific configuration paths (`kilo.json` instead of `opencode.json`, `~/.config/kilo/` instead of `~/.config/opencode/`). SessionStart hook availability depends on KiloCode's implementation.
387	
388	</details>
389	
390	<details>
391	<summary><strong>OpenClaw / Pi Agent</strong> — native gateway plugin</summary>
392	
393	**Prerequisites:** OpenClaw gateway running ([>2026.1.29](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/9761)), Node.js 22+.
394	
395	context-mode runs as a native [OpenClaw](https://github.com/openclaw) gateway plugin, targeting **Pi Agent** sessions (Read/Write/Edit/Bash tools). Unlike other platforms, there's no separate MCP server — the plugin registers directly into the gateway runtime via OpenClaw's [plugin API](https://docs.openclaw.ai/tools/plugin).
396	
397	**Install:**
398	
399	1. Clone and install:
400	
401	   ```bash
402	   git clone https://github.com/mksglu/context-mode.git
403	   cd context-mode
404	   npm run install:openclaw
405	   ```
406	
407	   The installer uses `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` from your environment (default: `/openclaw`). To specify a custom path:
408	
409	   ```bash
410	   npm run install:openclaw -- /path/to/openclaw-state
411	   ```
412	
413	   Common locations: **Docker** — `/openclaw` (the default). **Local** — `~/.openclaw` or wherever you set `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`.
414	
415	   The installer handles everything: `npm install`, `npm run build`, `better-sqlite3` native rebuild, extension registration in `runtime.json`, and gateway restart via SIGUSR1.
416	
417	2. Open a Pi Agent session.
418	
419	**Verify:** The plugin registers 8 hooks via [`api.on()`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/tools/plugin) (lifecycle) and [`api.registerHook()`](https://docs.openclaw.ai/tools/plugin) (commands). Type `ctx stats` to confirm tools are loaded.
420	
421	**Routing:** Automatic. All tool interception, session tracking, and compaction recovery hooks activate automatically — no manual hook configuration or routing file needed.
422	
423	> **Minimum version:** OpenClaw >2026.1.29 — this includes the `api.on()` lifecycle fix from [PR #9761](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/9761). On older versions, lifecycle hooks silently fail. The adapter falls back to DB snapshot reconstruction (less precise but preserves critical state).
424	
425	Full documentation: [`docs/adapters/openclaw.md`](docs/adapters/openclaw.md)
426	
427	</details>
428	
429	<details>
430	<summary><strong>Codex CLI</strong> — MCP + hooks (waiting for upstream dispatch)</summary>
431	
432	**Prerequisites:** Node.js 18+, Codex CLI installed.
433	
434	**Install:**
435	
436	1. Install context-mode globally:
437	
438	   ```bash
439	   npm install -g context-mode
440	   ```
441	
442	2. Add to `~/.codex/config.toml`:
443	
444	   ```toml
445	   [mcp_servers.context-mode]
446	   command = "context-mode"
447	   ```
448	
449	3. *(Waiting for upstream)* Enable the hooks feature flag. Add to `~/.codex/config.toml`:
450	
451	   ```toml
452	   [features]
453	   codex_hooks = true
454	   ```
455	
456	   > **Status:** Codex CLI's hook system is implemented in source (`codex-rs/hooks/`) but hook dispatch is not yet wired into the tool execution pipeline (`Stage::UnderDevelopment`). The feature flag is accepted but hooks don't fire during sessions as of v0.118.0. Our hook scripts are ready — they'll work immediately once Codex enables dispatch. Track progress: [openai/codex#16685](https://github.com/openai/codex/issues/16685).
457	
458	4. *(Prepare for when dispatch is enabled)* Add hooks for routing enforcement and session tracking. Create `~/.codex/hooks.json`:
459	
460	   ```json
461	   {
462	     "hooks": {
463	       "PreToolUse": [{ "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "context-mode hook codex pretooluse" }] }],
464	       "PostToolUse": [{ "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "context-mode hook codex posttooluse" }] }],
465	       "SessionStart": [{ "hooks": [{ "type": "command", "command": "context-mode hook codex sessionstart" }] }]
466	     }
467	   }
468	   ```
469	
470	   `PreToolUse` enforces sandbox routing (blocks dangerous commands, redirects to MCP tools). `PostToolUse` captures session events. `SessionStart` restores state after compaction.
471	
472	   > **Note:** PreToolUse routing supports deny rules only (blocks dangerous commands). Context injection (`additionalContext`) is not supported in Codex PreToolUse — it works via PostToolUse and SessionStart instead. This is handled automatically.
473	
474	5. Copy routing instructions (recommended even with hooks for full routing awareness):
475	
476	   ```bash
477	   cp node_modules/context-mode/configs/codex/AGENTS.md ./AGENTS.md
478	   ```
479	
480	   For global use: `cp node_modules/context-mode/configs/codex/AGENTS.md ~/.codex/AGENTS.md`. Global applies to all projects. If both exist, Codex CLI merges them.
481	
482	6. Restart Codex CLI.
483	
484	**Verify:** Start a session and type `ctx stats`. Context-mode tools should appear and respond.
485	
486	**Routing:** MCP tools work. Hook-based routing is ready but waiting for Codex to enable hook dispatch. The `AGENTS.md` file provides routing instructions for model awareness in the meantime.
487	
488	> **Exec mode regression (v0.118.0):** `codex exec` cancels all MCP tool calls with "user cancelled MCP tool call". The `tool_call_mcp_elicitation` flag went stable in 0.118.0, adding an approval prompt that exec-mode can't handle. **Pin to Codex ≤0.116.0 for exec-mode MCP.** Confirmed by upstream: [openai/codex#16685](https://github.com/openai/codex/issues/16685). Interactive mode (`codex` / `codex --full-auto`) is not affected.
489	
490	</details>
491	
492	<details>
493	<summary><strong>Antigravity</strong> — MCP-only, no hooks</summary>
494	
495	**Prerequisites:** Node.js 18+, Antigravity installed.
496	
497	**Install:**
498	
499	1. Install context-mode globally:
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/Users/shivpat/context-mode/README.md`

```text
550	     "mcpServers": {
551	       "context-mode": {
552	         "command": "context-mode"
553	       }
554	     }
555	   }
556	   ```
557	
558	3. Create `.kiro/hooks/context-mode.json`:
559	
560	   ```json
561	   {
562	     "name": "context-mode",
563	     "description": "Context-mode hooks for context window protection",
564	     "hooks": {
565	       "preToolUse": [
566	         { "matcher": "*", "command": "context-mode hook kiro pretooluse" }
567	       ],
568	       "postToolUse": [
569	         { "matcher": "*", "command": "context-mode hook kiro posttooluse" }
570	       ]
571	     }
572	   }
573	   ```
574	
575	4. Copy routing instructions. Kiro's `agentSpawn` (SessionStart) is not yet implemented, so the model needs a routing file at session start:
576	
577	   ```bash
578	   cp node_modules/context-mode/configs/kiro/KIRO.md ./KIRO.md
579	   ```
580	
581	5. Restart Kiro.
582	
583	**Verify:** Open the Kiro panel > MCP Servers tab and confirm "context-mode" shows a green status indicator. In chat, type `ctx stats`.
584	
585	**Routing:** Hooks enforce routing programmatically via `preToolUse`/`postToolUse`. The `KIRO.md` file provides routing instructions since `agentSpawn` (SessionStart equivalent) is not yet wired. Tool names appear as `@context-mode/ctx_batch_execute`, `@context-mode/ctx_search`, etc. Auto-detected via MCP protocol handshake.
586	
587	Full configs: [`configs/kiro/mcp.json`](configs/kiro/mcp.json) | [`configs/kiro/agent.json`](configs/kiro/agent.json) | [`configs/kiro/KIRO.md`](configs/kiro/KIRO.md)
588	
589	</details>
590	
591	<details>
592	<summary><strong>Zed</strong> — MCP-only, no hooks</summary>
593	
594	**Prerequisites:** Node.js 18+, Zed installed.
595	
596	**Install:**
597	
598	1. Install context-mode globally:
599	
600	   ```bash
601	   npm install -g context-mode
602	   ```
603	
604	2. Add to `~/.config/zed/settings.json` (Windows: `%APPDATA%\Zed\settings.json`):
605	
606	   ```json
607	   {
608	     "context_servers": {
609	       "context-mode": {
610	         "command": {
611	           "path": "context-mode"
612	         }
613	       }
614	     }
615	   }
616	   ```
617	
618	   Note: Zed uses `"context_servers"` and `"command": { "path": "..." }` syntax, not `"mcpServers"` or `"command": "..."` like other platforms.
619	
620	3. Copy routing instructions (Zed has no hook support):
621	
622	   ```bash
623	   cp node_modules/context-mode/configs/zed/AGENTS.md ./AGENTS.md
624	   ```
625	
626	4. Restart Zed (or save `settings.json` — Zed auto-restarts context servers on config change).
627	
628	**Verify:** Open the Agent Panel (`Cmd+Shift+A`), go to settings, and check the indicator dot next to "context-mode" — green means active. Type `ctx stats` in the agent chat.
629	
630	**Routing:** Manual. The `AGENTS.md` file is the only enforcement method (~60% compliance). There is no programmatic interception. Tool names appear as `mcp:context-mode:ctx_batch_execute`, `mcp:context-mode:ctx_search`, etc. Auto-detected via MCP protocol handshake.
631	
632	</details>
633	
634	<details>
635	<summary><strong>Pi Coding Agent</strong> — extension with full hook support</summary>
636	
637	**Prerequisites:** Node.js 18+, Pi Coding Agent installed.
638	
639	**Install:**
640	
641	1. Clone the extension:
642	
643	   ```bash
644	   git clone https://github.com/mksglu/context-mode.git ~/.pi/extensions/context-mode
645	   cd ~/.pi/extensions/context-mode
646	   npm install
647	   npm run build
648	   ```
649	
650	2. Add to `~/.pi/agent/mcp.json` (or `.pi/mcp.json` for project-level):
651	
652	   ```json
653	   {
654	     "mcpServers": {
655	       "context-mode": {
656	         "command": "node",
657	         "args": ["/home/youruser/.pi/extensions/context-mode/node_modules/context-mode/start.mjs"]
658	       }
659	     }
660	   }
661	   ```
662	
663	   > **Note:** JSON does not expand `~`. Replace `/home/youruser` with your actual home directory (run `echo $HOME` to find it).
664	
665	3. Restart Pi.
666	
667	**Verify:** In a Pi session, type `ctx stats`. Context-mode tools should appear and respond.
668	
669	**Routing:** Automatic. The extension registers all key lifecycle events (`tool_call`, `tool_result`, `session_start`, `session_before_compact`), providing full session continuity and routing enforcement.
670	
671	</details>
672	
673	<details>
674	<summary><strong>Build Prerequisites</strong> <sup>(CentOS, RHEL, Alpine)</sup></summary>
675	
676	Context Mode uses [better-sqlite3](https://github.com/WiseLibs/better-sqlite3) on Node.js, which ships prebuilt native binaries for most platforms. On glibc >= 2.31 systems (Ubuntu 20.04+, Debian 11+, Fedora 34+, macOS, Windows), `npm install` works without any build tools.
677	
678	**Linux + Node.js >= 22.13:** Context Mode automatically uses the built-in `node:sqlite` module instead of `better-sqlite3`. This eliminates the native addon entirely, avoiding [sporadic SIGSEGV crashes](https://github.com/nodejs/node/issues/62515) caused by V8's `madvise(MADV_DONTNEED)` corrupting the addon's `.got.plt` section on Linux. No configuration needed — detection is automatic. Falls back to `better-sqlite3` on older Node.js versions.
679	
680	**Bun users:** No native compilation needed. Context Mode automatically detects Bun and uses the built-in `bun:sqlite` module via a compatibility adapter. `better-sqlite3` and all its build dependencies are skipped entirely.
681	
682	On older glibc systems (CentOS 7/8, RHEL 8, Debian 10), prebuilt binaries don't load and better-sqlite3 **automatically falls back to compiling from source** via `prebuild-install || node-gyp rebuild --release`. This requires a C++20 compiler (GCC 10+), Make, and Python with setuptools.
683	
684	**CentOS 8 / RHEL 8** (glibc 2.28):
685	
686	```bash
687	dnf install -y gcc-toolset-10-gcc gcc-toolset-10-gcc-c++ make python3 python3-setuptools
688	scl enable gcc-toolset-10 'npm install -g context-mode'
689	```
690	
691	**CentOS 7 / RHEL 7** (glibc 2.17):
692	
693	```bash
694	yum install -y centos-release-scl
695	yum install -y devtoolset-10-gcc devtoolset-10-gcc-c++ make python3
696	pip3 install setuptools
697	scl enable devtoolset-10 'npm install -g context-mode'
698	```
699	
700	**Alpine Linux:**
701	
702	Alpine prebuilt binaries (musl) are available in better-sqlite3 v12.8.0+. With the `^12.6.2` dependency range, `npm install` resolves to the latest 12.x and works without build tools on Alpine. If you pin an older version:
703	
704	```bash
705	apk add build-base python3 py3-setuptools
706	npm install -g context-mode
707	```
708	
709	</details>
710	
711	## Tools
712	
713	| Tool | What it does | Context saved |
714	|---|---|---|
715	| `ctx_batch_execute` | Run multiple commands + search multiple queries in ONE call. | 986 KB → 62 KB |
716	| `ctx_execute` | Run code in 11 languages. Only stdout enters context. | 56 KB → 299 B |
717	| `ctx_execute_file` | Process files in sandbox. Raw content never leaves. | 45 KB → 155 B |
718	| `ctx_index` | Chunk markdown into FTS5 with BM25 ranking. | 60 KB → 40 B |
719	| `ctx_search` | Query indexed content with multiple queries in one call. | On-demand retrieval |
720	| `ctx_fetch_and_index` | Fetch URL, chunk and index. 24h TTL cache — repeat calls skip network. `force: true` to bypass. | 60 KB → 40 B |
721	| `ctx_stats` | Show context savings, call counts, and session statistics. | — |
722	| `ctx_doctor` | Diagnose installation: runtimes, hooks, FTS5, versions. | — |
723	| `ctx_upgrade` | Upgrade to latest version from GitHub, rebuild, reconfigure hooks. | — |
724	| `ctx_purge` | Permanently deletes all indexed content from the knowledge base. | — |
725	
726	## How the Sandbox Works
727	
728	Each `ctx_execute` call spawns an isolated subprocess with its own process boundary. Scripts can't access each other's memory or state. The subprocess runs your code, captures stdout, and only that stdout enters the conversation context. The raw data — log files, API responses, snapshots — never leaves the sandbox.
729	
730	Eleven language runtimes are available: JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Shell, Ruby, Go, Rust, PHP, Perl, R, and Elixir. Bun is auto-detected for 3-5x faster JS/TS execution.
731	
732	Authenticated CLIs work through credential passthrough — `gh`, `aws`, `gcloud`, `kubectl`, `docker` inherit environment variables and config paths without exposing them to the conversation.
733	
734	When output exceeds 5 KB and an `intent` is provided, Context Mode switches to intent-driven filtering: it indexes the full output into the knowledge base, searches for sections matching your intent, and returns only the relevant matches with a vocabulary of searchable terms for follow-up queries.
735	
736	## How the Knowledge Base Works
737	
738	The `ctx_index` tool chunks markdown content by headings while keeping code blocks intact, then stores them in a **SQLite FTS5** (Full-Text Search 5) virtual table. The SQLite backend is selected automatically at runtime: `bun:sqlite` on Bun, `node:sqlite` on Linux + Node.js >= 22.13, and `better-sqlite3` everywhere else. Search uses **BM25 ranking** — a probabilistic relevance algorithm that scores documents based on term frequency, inverse document frequency, and document length normalization. **Porter stemming** is applied at index time so "running", "runs", and "ran" match the same stem. Titles and headings are weighted **5x** in BM25 scoring for precise navigational queries.
739	
740	When you call `ctx_search`, it returns relevant content snippets focused around matching query terms — not full documents, not approximations, the actual indexed content with smart extraction around what you're looking for. `ctx_fetch_and_index` extends this to URLs: fetch, convert HTML to markdown, chunk, index. The raw page never enters context. Use the `contentType` parameter to filter results by type (e.g. `code` or `prose`).
741	
742	### Ranking: Reciprocal Rank Fusion
743	
744	Search runs two parallel strategies and merges them with **Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF)**:
745	
746	- **Porter stemming** — FTS5 MATCH with porter tokenizer. "caching" matches "cached", "caches", "cach".
747	- **Trigram substring** — FTS5 trigram tokenizer matches partial strings. "useEff" finds "useEffect", "authenticat" finds "authentication".
748	
749	RRF merges both ranked lists into a single result set, so a document that ranks well in both strategies surfaces higher than one that ranks well in only one. This replaces the old cascading fallback approach where trigram results were only used if porter returned nothing.
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-22 23:18:27Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-22 23:18:43Z)**_

Here is a direct assessment of where context-mode addresses real pain points in this workflow:

---

## Three concrete improvements for HarmonyForge

### 1. Stop dumping MusicXML/JSON into context on every read

MusicXML files are verbose XML — a single 4-voice SATB arrangement can easily be 40–100 KB. Right now, every time I read one, the full content enters the context window. With context-mode:

```
ctx_execute_file → process the file in sandbox → only the extracted notes/chords/violations return to context
```
**Impact:** A 60 KB MusicXML read becomes ~200 bytes of structured output.

---

### 2. Index the rules directory instead of loading it per-turn

The `.claude/rules/` directory (`hci-protocol.md`, `examples.md`, `output-completeness.md`) gets pulled into context on relevant turns. Instead:

```
ctx_index .claude/rules/**  →  ctx_search "output completeness rules"
```
Only the relevant section surfaces, not the full file. Same applies to `PROGRESS.md`, `notes/journey.md`, and `IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md` as they grow.

---

### 3. Session continuity across context compaction

This workflow is explicitly multi-phase with strict state tracking (`PROGRESS.md`, `CLAUDE.md` correction loop). When the conversation compacts mid-session, I currently lose which file I was editing and what phase we were in. Context-mode's SQLite session tracker indexes every file edit and git operation into FTS5 — on resume, it retrieves only what's relevant via BM25 search rather than dumping the full history back.

---

## How to actually install it for Claude Code

The plugin marketplace install is two commands:

```bash
/plugin marketplace add mksglu/context-mode
/plugin install context-mode@context-mode
```

Then restart and run `/context-mode:ctx-doctor` — all checks green means hooks are wired (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PreCompact, SessionStart). No configuration files needed; routing is injected at session start automatically.

---

**Critical trade-off to flag:** Context-mode's "Think in Code" paradigm — writing scripts instead of reading files — conflicts with the preview→gate→execute protocol in `CLAUDE.md`. If the sandbox executes analysis scripts autonomously, it bypasses the confirmation gate for any operation that touches file content. We'd need to explicitly scope context-mode to read-only analysis operations, not file edits.

> **Shall I proceed with the plugin install and scoping rules as described above? (Yes / No / Request changes)**

---

_**User (2026-04-22 23:19:11Z)**_

                                                        
  /plugin marketplace add mksglu/context-mode
  /plugin install context-mode@context-mode

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-22 23:19:50Z)**_

MCP server is now registered. This gives you the 6 sandbox tools (`ctx_execute`, `ctx_index`, `ctx_search`, etc.) immediately. The difference vs. the full plugin install is that **hooks won't auto-enforce routing** — the model uses the sandbox tools when asked, but won't be automatically redirected away from Bash/Read for large outputs.

To get the full hooks (automatic routing enforcement + session continuity on compaction), use Option A — type `/plugin marketplace add mksglu/context-mode` directly in the Claude Code prompt bar, then `/plugin install context-mode@context-mode`.

Verify the MCP server is live by typing `ctx stats` in your next prompt, Superman.
